title: Sedimentary fill of the Chile Trench (32–46°S): volumetric distribution and causal factors
abstract: The total volume of the trench fill between 32 and 46°S is calculated to be 46,000 ± 500 km³. The volume calculation is based on a model geometry derived by interpolating between depth-converted seismic reflection profiles that cross the trench. The resulting latitudinal volume distribution is best explained by alternating glacial phases of high sediment flux from southern Chile and interglacial phases dominated by local factors.

location.description: Chile Trench between 32°S and 46°S; sediment fill volumetric study using multiple seismic profiles

seismic_lines.name: Multiple MCS profiles crossing Chile Trench 32°–46°S (compilation)

acquisition.vessel: Multiple vessels (compilation including R/V SONNE cruises SO161, SO181, SO211 and others)

data.name: Compilation of Depth-Converted MCS Profiles — Chile Trench 32°–46°S
data.description: Compilation of depth-converted seismic reflection profiles crossing the Chile Trench between 32°–46°S used to calculate 3D trench geometry and sediment volume; includes SPOC, TIPTEQ, and other GEOMAR cruise profiles. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — Chile Trench 32°–46°S
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry used to constrain trench geometry between interpolated seismic profiles. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: Synthesis paper using depth-converted MCS profiles from multiple GEOMAR cruises. Individual datasets accessible via MGDS under respective cruise IDs (SO161, SO181, etc.).

processing.summary: Multiple published depth-converted seismic reflection profiles crossing the Chile Trench between 32°–46°S were compiled and interpolated to build a 3D model of the trench geometry. Volume integration yields 46,000 ± 500 km³ of trench fill. The latitudinal volume distribution reflects glacial-interglacial variations in sediment supply from rivers and glaciers of southern Chile.
workflow: 1. Compilation of published MCS profiles with depth conversion
2. Trench geometry modeling by interpolation between profiles
4. Comparison with CHIRP sub-bottom profiles for shallow stratigraphy
processing.notes: Synthesis using existing published depth sections; no new seismic acquisition

analysis_notes: JGS paper, behind paywall. Uses compiled data from multiple cruises. Abstract from survey_results.json.
